title: Composition Candidates To Add
description: New compositions (frameworks, services, protocols, applications) surfaced during scans or pattern authoring but not yet catalogued in compositions-src/. The symmetric analog of pattern-todo.json for the composition layer. Distinct from compositions-todo.json (a pattern-centric usage-coverage worklist). Each entry names a candidate composition, the patterns it would implement, and the upstream evidence with a verifiable url and verbatim quote. needs_verification:true means the candidate was surfaced from a secondary mention and must have its own primary source content-verified before authoring. Entries graduate by being authored into compositions-src/ and removed from this list.

candidates.name: Merchant Payments Protocol (MPP)
candidates.summary: An agentic payments protocol compared alongside ACP, AP2, and x402 in protocol round-ups. Likely implements the agent-initiated payment and verifiable purchase mandate shapes, but its own primary specification has not yet been content-verified.

suggested_members.note: Expected: an agent settling a payment under a delegated authorization.
Expected: signed authorization carried with the transaction.

raised_by.note: Named in a content-verified agentic-payments protocol comparison alongside ACP/AP2/x402. The comparison page was verified, but MPP's own spec/docs were not — resolve needs_verification by reading the MPP primary source before authoring.

evidence.title: Agentic payments protocols compared: MPP, ACP, AP2, x402 (Crossmint)
evidence.quote: Agentic payments protocols compared: Which is best for your AI agents? (MPP, ACP, AP2, x402)

candidates.name: Moshi (Kyutai)
candidates.summary: A full-duplex speech LLM from Kyutai that handles turn-taking natively, letting user and agent speak simultaneously without a silence-threshold turn boundary. Referenced as a known use of the semantic-turn-endpointing pattern but not yet catalogued as its own composition.

suggested_members.note: Native full-duplex turn-taking instead of a VAD silence threshold.

raised_by.note: Listed as a known_use of the semantic-turn-endpointing pattern. Verify a Kyutai Moshi primary source (paper or repo) and confirm a second member pattern before authoring.

evidence.title: Turn-Taking in Voice Agents: Why Rule-Based VAD Is Broken and What Comes Next (Gradium)
